payload.markdown:
# Covenant Compliance Memo — ACME INDUSTRIES S.A.S.

## Executive summary
ACME INDUSTRIES S.A.S. is in confirmed breach of the Leverage Ratio covenant as of Q2 2026, reporting 3.70x against a maximum threshold of 3.50x [Credit Agreement, Section 7.1(a)]. While Minimum Liquidity remains compliant at EUR 5.3m following the supersession of preliminary data, the Interest Coverage Ratio is trending downward and projected to breach by Q4 2026.

## Findings

| Covenant | Required | Actual | Verdict | Confidence |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Leverage Ratio | <= 3.50x | 3.70x | **Breach** | 98% |
| Minimum Liquidity | >= EUR 5.0m | EUR 5.3m | OK | 100% |
| Interest Coverage | >= 2.00x | 2.50x | At Risk | 90% |

## Root cause
The Leverage Ratio breach is primarily driven by a EUR 40.0m draw on Facility B to fund the Project Falcon acquisition of TargetCo GmbH [Debt Movement Schedule — Quarter Ended 30 June 2026]. Additionally, an undocumented EUR 5.0m draw from a short-term bilateral facility with Banque du Nord contributed to the debt increase, accounting for the unexplained portion of the leverage spike [Debt Movement Schedule — Quarter Ended 30 June 2026]. These debt increases were not offset by proportional growth in LTM EBITDA, which remains at EUR 100.0m [Note 4 — Alternative Performance Measures (LTM Reconciliation)].

## Eliminated false positives
The initial flagging of a Minimum Liquidity breach was overturned. The analyzer incorrectly utilized a preliminary flash figure of EUR 4.2m; however, per Section 9.4, preliminary estimates are superseded by final figures for covenant purposes [Credit Agreement, Section 9.4 Measurement; Supersession]. The final reported unrestricted cash of EUR 5.3m confirms compliance with the >= EUR 5.0m requirement [Liquidity and Capital Resources].

## Early warnings
The Interest Coverage Ratio has deteriorated consecutively from 3.10x (Q4 2025) to 2.50x (Q2 2026) [Note 4 — Alternative Performance Measures (LTM Reconciliation)]. At the current trajectory of -0.30x per period, the ratio is projected to fall below the 2.00x covenant floor within approximately 1.7 periods, targeting a breach in Q4 2026.

## Recommended actions
1.  **Invoke Equity Cure:** Immediately notify the Lender of the intent to cure the Leverage Ratio breach under Section 10.2 by injecting qualifying equity to reduce Total Debt within 20 Business Days of the compliance certificate delivery [Credit Agreement, Section 10.2 Equity Cure].
2.  **Formal Notification:** Issue a formal notice of Default pursuant to Section 7.3, detailing the breach and the specific remedial steps (equity injection) being undertaken [Credit Agreement, Section 7.3 Notification of Default].
3.  **Address Undocumented Debt:** Provide immediate documentation or repayment plans for the EUR 5.0m Banque du Nord facility to ensure Total Debt calculations align with defined Financial Indebtedness [Credit Agreement, Section 1. Definitions].
4.  **Mitigation Planning:** Develop a liquidity preservation plan to arrest the decline in Interest Coverage, as a second covenant breach would constitute an Event of Default under Section 10.1 [Credit Agreement, Section 10.1 Financial Covenant Default].

---
*Methodology: every figure was computed by deterministic tools, never by the language model. Confidence per finding = 80% weighted Critic checks (citation validity 30%, data freshness 25%, definition basis 25%, internal consistency 20%) + 20% cause coverage — the share of the flagged movement matched to clearly documented causes. At-risk verdicts based on a trend projection carry an additional 0.9 projection factor. Overall confidence is the weakest actionable finding.*

sections.title: CREDIT AGREEMENT
sections.text:
Dated as of 12 March 2024

BETWEEN:
ACME INDUSTRIES S.A.S., a societe par actions simplifiee incorporated in France (the "Borrower")
AND
BANQUE LUTECE S.A. (the "Lender")

FACILITY: EUR 50,000,000 SENIOR TERM LOAN FACILITY ("Facility B")

sections.title: SECTION 1. DEFINITIONS
sections.text:
"EBITDA" means consolidated earnings before interest, tax, depreciation and amortisation of the Group, calculated in accordance with the Accounting Principles.

"LTM EBITDA" means EBITDA for the trailing twelve-month period ending on the relevant Quarter Date, as reconciled in the Borrower's quarterly reporting (alternative performance measures note). Quarterly (three-month) EBITDA figures shall not be used for covenant testing.

"Total Debt" means the aggregate outstanding principal amount of all financial indebtedness of the Group, including bank facilities, notes, bilateral loans and finance leases.

"Net Interest Expense" means interest payable on Total Debt less interest income, measured on a trailing twelve-month basis.

"Leverage Ratio" means, on any Quarter Date, the ratio of Total Debt to LTM EBITDA.

"Interest Coverage Ratio" means, on any Quarter Date, the ratio of LTM EBITDA to Net Interest Expense.

"Liquidity" means unrestricted cash and cash equivalents of the Group.

"Quarter Date" means 31 March, 30 June, 30 September and 31 December in each year.

sections.title: Section 7.1 Financial Covenants. The Borrower shall ensure that:
sections.text:
(a) Leverage Ratio. The Leverage Ratio on each Quarter Date shall not exceed 3.50:1.00.

(b) Minimum Liquidity. Liquidity on each Quarter Date shall not be less than EUR 5,000,000.

(c) Interest Coverage. The Interest Coverage Ratio on each Quarter Date shall not be less than 2.00:1.00.

sections.title: Section 7.2 Financial Reporting.
sections.text: The Borrower shall deliver to the Lender, within 45 days after each Quarter Date, a compliance certificate setting out computations of the financial covenants in reasonable detail.

sections.title: Section 7.3 Notification of Default.
sections.text: The Borrower shall notify the Lender of any Default, and the steps being taken to remedy it, promptly upon becoming aware of it.

sections.title: Section 9.4 Measurement; Supersession.
sections.text: Financial covenants are tested by reference to the final figures reported as at the relevant Quarter Date. Preliminary, flash or interim estimates circulated before publication of the final quarter-end figures are superseded by the final figures for all covenant purposes.

sections.title: Section 10.1 Financial Covenant Default.
sections.text: Failure to comply with any covenant in Section 7.1 constitutes an Event of Default, subject to Section 10.2.

sections.title: Section 10.2 Equity Cure.
sections.text: The Borrower may cure a breach of Section 7.1(a) no more than twice over the life of the Facility by way of a qualifying equity contribution applied to the reduction of Total Debt within 20 Business Days of delivery of the relevant compliance certificate.

sections.title: HIGHLIGHTS
sections.text:
Revenue for the quarter was EUR 118.4 million (Q1 2026: EUR 121.9 million).
EBITDA for the quarter was EUR 25.0 million (Q1 2026: EUR 24.5 million).
The Group completed the acquisition of TargetCo GmbH ("Project Falcon") in May 2026, financed primarily through an acquisition draw under Term Loan B.

sections.title: CONSOLIDATED BALANCE SHEET (SUMMARY)
sections.text:
Total financial indebtedness at 30 June 2026: EUR 370.0 million (31 March 2026: EUR 325.0 million; 31 December 2025: EUR 318.0 million).
Unrestricted cash and cash equivalents at 30 June 2026 (final): EUR 5.3 million.

sections.title: LIQUIDITY AND CAPITAL RESOURCES
sections.text:
Final unrestricted cash at the quarter end was EUR 5.3 million. This figure supersedes the preliminary treasury flash estimate circulated on 15 June 2026.
Net Interest Expense (trailing twelve months) at 30 June 2026: EUR 40.0 million (31 March 2026: EUR 36.4 million; 31 December 2025: EUR 33.5 million). The increase reflects the additional debt drawn for Project Falcon.

sections.title: NOTE 4 — ALTERNATIVE PERFORMANCE MEASURES (LTM RECONCILIATION)
sections.text:
LTM EBITDA (trailing twelve months):
- at 31 December 2025: EUR 104.0 million
- at 31 March 2026: EUR 102.0 million
- at 30 June 2026: EUR 100.0 million

LTM EBITDA is the covenant-relevant measure under the Group's credit documentation; quarterly EBITDA is disclosed for information only.

sections.title: NOTE 7 — SUBSEQUENT EVENTS
sections.text: No material subsequent events between 30 June 2026 and the date of this report.

sections.title: ACME INDUSTRIES S.A.S. — TREASURY PACK — Q2 2026
sections.text: Prepared by Group Treasury for the lender relationship file

sections.text:
Unrestricted cash position (estimate at 13 June 2026): EUR 4.2 million.
Status: preliminary flash estimate, before quarter-end collections. Subject to supersession by final quarter-end figures.

sections.title: DEBT MOVEMENT SCHEDULE — QUARTER ENDED 30 JUNE 2026

Opening Total Debt at 31 March 2026: EUR 325.0 million.

New drawings during the quarter:
- 12 May 2026 — EUR 40.0 million — Term Loan B acquisition draw — Project Falcon (acquisition of TargetCo GmbH) — Board approval BM-2026-04 — fully documented.
- 03 June 2026 — EUR 5.0 million — Short-term bilateral facility, Banque du Nord — stated purpose: general corporate purposes — no board memo reference on file.

Repayments during the quarter: none.

Closing Total Debt at 30 June 2026: EUR 370.0 million.

- 28 June 2026 — EUR 2.2 million — quarterly interest payment, Facility B.

"LTM EBITDA" means EBITDA for the trailing twelve-month period ending on the relevant Quarter Date, as reconciled in the Borrower's quarterly reporting (alternative performance measures note). Quarterly (three-month) EBITDA figures shall not be used for covenant testing.

"Total Debt" means the aggregate outstanding principal amount of all financial indebtedness of the Group, including bank facilities, notes, bilateral loans and finance leases.

"Net Interest Expense" means interest payable on Total Debt less interest income, measured on a trailing twelve-month basis.

"Leverage Ratio" means, on any Quarter Date, the ratio of Total Debt to LTM EBITDA.

"Interest Coverage Ratio" means, on any Quarter Date, the ratio of LTM EBITDA to Net Interest Expense.

"Liquidity" means unrestricted cash and cash equivalents of the Group.

"Quarter Date" means 31 March, 30 June, 30 September and 31 December in each year.

Final unrestricted cash at the quarter end was EUR 5.3 million. This figure supersedes the preliminary treasury flash estimate circulated on 15 June 2026.
Net Interest Expense (trailing twelve months) at 30 June 2026: EUR 40.0 million (31 March 2026: EUR 36.4 million; 31 December 2025: EUR 33.5 million). The increase reflects the additional debt drawn for Project Falcon.

Revenue for the quarter was EUR 118.4 million (Q1 2026: EUR 121.9 million).
EBITDA for the quarter was EUR 25.0 million (Q1 2026: EUR 24.5 million).
The Group completed the acquisition of TargetCo GmbH ("Project Falcon") in May 2026, financed primarily through an acquisition draw under Term Loan B.

The Borrower may cure a breach of Section 7.1(a) no more than twice over the life of the Facility by way of a qualifying equity contribution applied to the reduction of Total Debt within 20 Business Days of delivery of the relevant compliance certificate.
Failure to comply with any covenant in Section 7.1 constitutes an Event of Default, subject to Section 10.2.
The Borrower shall notify the Lender of any Default, and the steps being taken to remedy it, promptly upon becoming aware of it.

"EBITDA" means consolidated earnings before interest, tax, depreciation and amortisation of the Group, calculated in accordance with the Accounting Principles.

"LTM EBITDA" means EBITDA for the trailing twelve-month period ending on the relevant Quarter Date, as reconciled in the Borrower's quarterly reporting (alternative performance measures note). Quarterly (three-month) EBITDA figures shall not be used for covenant testing.

"Total Debt" means the aggregate outstanding principal amount of all financial indebtedness of the Group, including bank facilities, notes, bilateral loans and finance leases.

"Net Interest Expense" means interest payable on Total Debt less interest income, measured on a trailing twelve-month basis.

"Leverage Ratio" means, on any Quarter Date, the ratio of Total Debt to LTM EBITDA.

"Interest Coverage Ratio" means, on any Quarter Date, the ratio of LTM EBITDA to Net Interest Expense.

"Liquidity" means unrestricted cash and cash equivalents of the Group.

"Quarter Date" means 31 March, 30 June, 30 September and 31 December in each year.

Financial covenants are tested by reference to the final figures reported as at the relevant Quarter Date. Preliminary, flash or interim estimates circulated before publication of the final quarter-end figures are superseded by the final figures for all covenant purposes.

"LTM EBITDA" means EBITDA for the trailing twelve-month period ending on the relevant Quarter Date, as reconciled in the Borrower's quarterly reporting (alternative performance measures note). Quarterly (three-month) EBITDA figures shall not be used for covenant testing.

"Total Debt" means the aggregate outstanding principal amount of all financial indebtedness of the Group, including bank facilities, notes, bilateral loans and finance leases.

"Liquidity" means unrestricted cash and cash equivalents of the Group.

The Borrower shall deliver to the Lender, within 45 days after each Quarter Date, a compliance certificate setting out computations of the financial covenants in reasonable detail.

snippets.section: Section 7.1 Financial Covenants. The Borrower shall ensure that:
snippets.text:
(a) Leverage Ratio. The Leverage Ratio on each Quarter Date shall not exceed 3.50:1.00.

(b) Minimum Liquidity. Liquidity on each Quarter Date shall not be less than EUR 5,000,000.

(c) Interest Coverage. The Interest Coverage Ratio on each Quarter Date shall not be less than 2.00:1.00.

The Analyzer correctly selected the Q2-2026 final Total Debt (370.0) and LTM EBITDA (100.0). No fresher or superseding figures are available in the provided facts for the Q2-2026 test date.

The covenant requires 'LTM EBITDA' and 'final figures'. The Analyzer used the fact labeled 'basis=ltm' for EBITDA and 'basis=final' for Debt, strictly adhering to the definition: 'LTM EBITDA means EBITDA for the trailing twelve-month period... Quarterly (three-month) EBITDA figures shall not be used.'
The calculation 370.0 / 100.0 = 3.70x is mathematically correct. The conclusion that 3.70x > 3.50x constitutes a breach is logically sound based on the provided inputs.

verdicts.rationale: The Analyzer's finding is robust. The inputs match the governing definitions for basis (LTM vs Quarterly) and status (Final). The arithmetic is correct, and the resulting ratio of 3.70x clearly exceeds the 3.50x threshold. While Section 10.2 mentions an equity cure, the existence of a cure mechanism does not negate the initial breach status at the time of testing; it merely provides a remedy. Therefore, the 'breach' status stands.

The covenant requires testing on 'final figures'. The corrected finding uses the final figure of 5.3m, satisfying the basis requirement.

The corrected calculation (5.3m >= 5.0m) yields a pass ('ok'). The draft's conclusion of 'conflict' was based on an erroneous application of the supersession clause, creating a false conflict between a valid final number and an invalid preliminary number.

verdicts.rationale: The Analyzer erroneously applied a 'prudent provisional' logic to select the lower preliminary figure (4.2m) over the higher final figure (5.3m). Section 9.4 explicitly states that preliminary estimates are 'superseded by the final figures for all covenant purposes.' Therefore, the only valid data point is the final figure of 5.3m. Since 5.3m >= 5.0m, the covenant is met, and the status is 'ok', not 'conflict' or 'breach'.

state.memo_markdown:
# Covenant Compliance Memo — ACME INDUSTRIES S.A.S.

## Executive summary
ACME INDUSTRIES S.A.S. is in confirmed breach of the Leverage Ratio covenant as of Q2 2026, reporting 3.70x against a maximum threshold of 3.50x [Credit Agreement, Section 7.1(a)]. While Minimum Liquidity remains compliant at EUR 5.3m following the supersession of preliminary data, the Interest Coverage Ratio is trending downward and projected to breach by Q4 2026.

## Findings

| Covenant | Required | Actual | Verdict | Confidence |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Leverage Ratio | <= 3.50x | 3.70x | **Breach** | 98% |
| Minimum Liquidity | >= EUR 5.0m | EUR 5.3m | OK | 100% |
| Interest Coverage | >= 2.00x | 2.50x | At Risk | 90% |

## Root cause
The Leverage Ratio breach is primarily driven by a EUR 40.0m draw on Facility B to fund the Project Falcon acquisition of TargetCo GmbH [Debt Movement Schedule — Quarter Ended 30 June 2026]. Additionally, an undocumented EUR 5.0m draw from a short-term bilateral facility with Banque du Nord contributed to the debt increase, accounting for the unexplained portion of the leverage spike [Debt Movement Schedule — Quarter Ended 30 June 2026]. These debt increases were not offset by proportional growth in LTM EBITDA, which remains at EUR 100.0m [Note 4 — Alternative Performance Measures (LTM Reconciliation)].

## Eliminated false positives
The initial flagging of a Minimum Liquidity breach was overturned. The analyzer incorrectly utilized a preliminary flash figure of EUR 4.2m; however, per Section 9.4, preliminary estimates are superseded by final figures for covenant purposes [Credit Agreement, Section 9.4 Measurement; Supersession]. The final reported unrestricted cash of EUR 5.3m confirms compliance with the >= EUR 5.0m requirement [Liquidity and Capital Resources].

## Early warnings
The Interest Coverage Ratio has deteriorated consecutively from 3.10x (Q4 2025) to 2.50x (Q2 2026) [Note 4 — Alternative Performance Measures (LTM Reconciliation)]. At the current trajectory of -0.30x per period, the ratio is projected to fall below the 2.00x covenant floor within approximately 1.7 periods, targeting a breach in Q4 2026.

## Recommended actions
1.  **Invoke Equity Cure:** Immediately notify the Lender of the intent to cure the Leverage Ratio breach under Section 10.2 by injecting qualifying equity to reduce Total Debt within 20 Business Days of the compliance certificate delivery [Credit Agreement, Section 10.2 Equity Cure].
2.  **Formal Notification:** Issue a formal notice of Default pursuant to Section 7.3, detailing the breach and the specific remedial steps (equity injection) being undertaken [Credit Agreement, Section 7.3 Notification of Default].
3.  **Address Undocumented Debt:** Provide immediate documentation or repayment plans for the EUR 5.0m Banque du Nord facility to ensure Total Debt calculations align with defined Financial Indebtedness [Credit Agreement, Section 1. Definitions].
4.  **Mitigation Planning:** Develop a liquidity preservation plan to arrest the decline in Interest Coverage, as a second covenant breach would constitute an Event of Default under Section 10.1 [Credit Agreement, Section 10.1 Financial Covenant Default].

---
*Methodology: every figure was computed by deterministic tools, never by the language model. Confidence per finding = 80% weighted Critic checks (citation validity 30%, data freshness 25%, definition basis 25%, internal consistency 20%) + 20% cause coverage — the share of the flagged movement matched to clearly documented causes. At-risk verdicts based on a trend projection carry an additional 0.9 projection factor. Overall confidence is the weakest actionable finding.*
